mission.title: Install and Configure Copa on Kubernetes
mission.description: Copa is a CLI tool that allows you to directly patch container images without the need for full rebuilds, addressing critical security vulnerabilities quickly. Installing Copa enables you to enhance your container security posture by applying patches efficiently.

steps.title: Create Namespace
steps.description: Create a dedicated namespace for Copa to run in. This helps in organizing your Kubernetes resources.

steps.title: Apply Copa Deployment
steps.description: Deploy Copa using the provided Kubernetes manifests from the GitHub repository. Replace `<version>` with the desired version of Copa.

steps.title: Apply Copa Service
steps.description: Expose Copa using a service to allow access to the CLI tool.

steps.title: Verify Installation
steps.description: Check if the Copa pods are running correctly in the namespace.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Copa will show the Copa pod running in the specified namespace. You can also verify its functionality by executing a patch command on a container image.

uninstall.title: Remove Copa Resources
uninstall.description: To uninstall Copa, delete the deployment and service, and then remove the namespace.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Copa
upgrade.description: To upgrade Copa, apply the new version of the deployment and service manifests. Ensure to replace `<new-version>` with the latest version.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Running
troubleshooting.description: If the Copa pod is not in a Running state, check the pod logs for errors.

troubleshooting.title: Service Not Accessible
troubleshooting.description: If you cannot access the Copa service, ensure that the service is correctly configured and check the endpoints.

troubleshooting.title: Namespace Issues
troubleshooting.description: If you encounter issues related to the namespace, verify that it was created successfully and that all resources are deployed within it.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have kubectl installed and configured to communicate with your Kubernetes cluster.
